text.title: Streaming Response Ux Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Streaming Response Ux application MUST enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Cancel button MUST be visible from token 1, never appear mid-stream.
rule.rationale: Users who can't cancel a runaway response lose trust faster than any other UX failure (Vercel AI SDK postmortem 2024).

rule.statement: Time-to-first-token (TTFT) MUST be < 1.5s p95. If exceeded, show a streaming-prep placeholder, not a spinner.
Disable input + send while a stream is active OR explicitly support edit-during-stream with stream-abort + replay semantics.
rule.rationale: Two streams overlapping is the #1 source of LLM-UX bug reports; either prevent or commit fully.

rule.statement: Copy-button MUST work on the partial buffer; users copy mid-stream constantly.
rule.rationale: Copilot, ChatGPT, Claude.ai all support this. Withholding copy = perceived bug.

rule.statement: Auto-scroll-to-bottom ON by default but disengages on any manual scroll; resume button on scroll back.
rule.rationale: Forced scroll on long streams loses the user's place; total scroll lock loses the latest token. Toggle pattern is the only correct middle.
